ground_truth_internal_diagram_a.relevant_history: The patient has a history of substance abuse and has been through rehab to overcome it. He has had issues with his family, particularly with his mother, where he has felt rejected and emotionally neglected. He has struggled with obesity since childhood, which has affected his self-esteem and body image. He also has a history of being victimized and bullied due to his weight.
ground_truth_internal_diagram_a.core_beliefs: I am trapped. | I am out of control. | I am unlovable. | I am undesirable, unwanted.
ground_truth_internal_diagram_a.intermediate_beliefs:
Helpless: I'm just not very good at handling stress and I have poor self-control, which is why I need to not put myself in stressful situations. 
 Unlovable: If I show my true self, people will reject me the way my mother rejected me.
Depressive variant: Helpless: There's nothing I can do to change my situation. I cannot control myself. 
Unlovable: I don't deserve to be happy, so why even try to stay clean?
ground_truth_internal_diagram_a.coping_strategies: The patient has adopted avoidance as a coping strategy by distancing himself from his family to reduce exposure to negativity and conflict. He has started scheduling pleasant activities and planning his day ahead of time in order to maintain a sense of control over his emotions and circumstances.

ground_truth_internal_diagram_b.relevant_history: Patient has been experiencing frustration, anxiety, and anger related to familial relationships. This is significantly influenced by a conversation regarding his wedding list with his parents, leading to insights about his feelings and actions similar to his father's pattern of seeking approval and unconditional love from family members who do not provide it.
ground_truth_internal_diagram_b.core_beliefs: I am powerless, weak, vulnerable. | I am trapped. | I am unlovable. | I am undesirable, unwanted. | I am bound to be abandoned. | I am bound to be alone.
ground_truth_internal_diagram_b.intermediate_beliefs:
Patient holds the belief that one must continuously seek familial approval and support to feel safe and valued. Patient also believes that investing in unhealthy familial relationships is necessary despite ongoing distress and frustration.
Depressive variant: During periods of low mood, the patient likely adopts more polarized views on family loyalty and support, feeling an increased sense of obligation to pursue approval, which exacerbates feelings of helplessness and rejection.
ground_truth_internal_diagram_b.coping_strategies: Patient tends to engage in conversations with family members about emotional support and seeks to change family dynamics, though this is often met with resistance.
